prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(record-intake collection_batch_1)
(reserve-drying-unit-for-subject collection_batch_1 drying_unit_a)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(confirm-quality-inspection collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(reserve-drying-channel-for-collection-batch collection_batch_1 drying_channel_1 moisture_meter_1)
(allocate-conditioning-equipment-to-subject collection_batch_1 grader_1)
(mark-collection-batch-ready-for-allocation collection_batch_1 drying_channel_1 grader_1)
(record-intake farm_batch_1)
(reserve-drying-unit-for-subject farm_batch_1 drying_unit_b)
(release-inspection-device collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject farm_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(confirm-quality-inspection farm_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(reserve-time-window-for-farm-batch farm_batch_1 time_window_1 moisture_meter_1)
(release-conditioning-equipment collection_batch_1 grader_1)
(allocate-conditioning-equipment-to-subject farm_batch_1 grader_1)
(confirm-farm-batch-conditioning farm_batch_1 time_window_1 grader_1)
(release-inspection-device farm_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(materialize-unit-instance-reservation collection_batch_1 farm_batch_1 drying_channel_1 time_window_1 drying_unit_instance_1)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(mark-unit-instance-ready-for-configuration drying_unit_instance_1 collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(release-inspection-device collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(record-intake processing_order_1)
(reserve-drying-unit-for-subject processing_order_1 drying_unit_c)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject processing_order_1 moisture_meter_1)
(confirm-quality-inspection processing_order_1 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-drying-profile-to-order processing_order_1 drying_profile_default)
(attach-regulatory-document-to-order processing_order_1 regulatory_doc_1)
(reserve-commodity-grade-for-unit-instance processing_order_1 grade_a drying_unit_instance_1)
(preconfigure-order-for-unit-instance processing_order_1 grade_a drying_unit_instance_1 moisture_meter_1)
(release-inspection-device processing_order_1 moisture_meter_1)
(allocate-conditioning-equipment-to-subject processing_order_1 grader_1)
(complete-order-configuration-without-profile-requirement processing_order_1 drying_profile_default grader_1 grade_a drying_unit_instance_1)
(release-conditioning-equipment processing_order_1 grader_1)
(authorize-order-without-packing-or-profile-requirements processing_order_1 regulatory_doc_1 grade_a drying_unit_instance_1)
(finalize-order-execution-without-outbound-requirement processing_order_1)
(release-drying-profile-from-order processing_order_1 drying_profile_default)
(detach-regulatory-document-from-order processing_order_1 regulatory_doc_1)
(record-intake processing_order_2)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject processing_order_1 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-distribution-slot-to-subject processing_order_1 distribution_slot_1 moisture_meter_1)
(place-processing-order-into-drying-unit processing_order_1 drying_unit_c distribution_slot_1)
(release-inspection-device processing_order_1 moisture_meter_1)
(reserve-drying-unit-for-subject processing_order_2 drying_unit_c)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject processing_order_2 moisture_meter_1)
(confirm-quality-inspection processing_order_2 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-drying-profile-to-order processing_order_2 drying_profile_default)
(attach-regulatory-document-to-order processing_order_2 regulatory_doc_1)
(reserve-commodity-grade-for-unit-instance processing_order_2 grade_b drying_unit_instance_1)
(preconfigure-order-for-unit-instance processing_order_2 grade_b drying_unit_instance_1 moisture_meter_1)
(release-inspection-device processing_order_2 moisture_meter_1)
(allocate-conditioning-equipment-to-subject processing_order_2 grader_1)
(complete-order-configuration-without-profile-requirement processing_order_2 drying_profile_default grader_1 grade_b drying_unit_instance_1)
(release-conditioning-equipment processing_order_2 grader_1)
(authorize-order-without-packing-or-profile-requirements processing_order_2 regulatory_doc_1 grade_b drying_unit_instance_1)
(finalize-order-execution-without-outbound-requirement processing_order_2)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject processing_order_2 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-distribution-slot-to-subject processing_order_2 distribution_slot_1 moisture_meter_1)
(place-processing-order-into-drying-unit processing_order_2 drying_unit_c distribution_slot_1)
(release-inspection-device processing_order_2 moisture_meter_1)
(release-collection-batch-for-distribution collection_batch_1 drying_unit_instance_1)
(release-farm-batch-for-distribution farm_batch_1 drying_unit_instance_1)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-distribution-slot-to-subject collection_batch_1 distribution_slot_1 moisture_meter_1)
(place-collection-batch-into-drying-unit collection_batch_1 drying_unit_a distribution_slot_1)
(release-inspection-device collection_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(allocate-inspection-device-to-subject farm_batch_1 moisture_meter_1)
(assign-distribution-slot-to-subject farm_batch_1 distribution_slot_1 moisture_meter_1)
(place-farm-batch-into-drying-unit farm_batch_1 drying_unit_b distribution_slot_1)
```

## Explanation of Generated Plan

The provided plan achieves the goal by systematically processing each workflow subject (collection_batch_1, farm_batch_1, processing_order_1, and processing_order_2) through the required preconditions, managing shared resources like inspection_device (moisture_meter_1), conditioning_equipment (grader_1), drying units, and other limited assets.

### Key Phases:
1. **Prepare collection_batch_1**: Intake registration, drying unit reservation (drying_unit_a), inspection, channel reservation, conditioning, and readiness confirmation.
2. **Prepare farm_batch_1**: Similar process using drying_unit_b and time_window_1.
3. **Reserve drying_unit_instance_1**: Using materialized reservation after both batches are ready.
4. **Configure drying_unit_instance_1**: Mark ready for configuration.
5. **Process processing_order_1**: Intake, reservation (drying_unit_c), inspection, resource assignments (drying profile, regulatory document, commodity grade grade_a), preconfiguration, configuration completion, authorization, and finalization to marked-for-release. Place it to free drying_unit_c.
6. **Process processing_order_2**: Reuse freed drying_unit_c, similar steps with grade_b. Place it.
7. **Release batches for distribution**: Mark collection_batch_1 and farm_batch_1 for release using the configured unit instance.
8. **Place batches**: Sequence placement, reusing distribution_slot_1.

### Resource Management:
- **Single inspection_device**: Allocated/released as needed between subjects.
- **Single conditioning_equipment**: Similarly managed.
- **Drying profiles and regulatory documents**: Released from processing_order_1 before use by processing_order_2.
- **Drying units**: Reused by placing processing_order_1 before reserving for processing_order_2.
- **Distribution slot**: Reused after each placement.
- **No treatment material, packing specs, outbound channels, or client contracts used**, as the selected path (without packing/profile requirements or outbound) avoids them.

This plan is minimal, avoids unnecessary actions, and satisfies all preconditions without violations.
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many predicates and actions. The goal is to place collection_batch_1, farm_batch_1, processing_order_1, and processing_order_2 into drying units.


## Examining initial state  
- The task involves managing collections and farm batches, specifically collection_batch_1, farm_batch_1, and processing orders processing_order_1 and processing_order_2.  
- The goal is to allocate these items into drying units, indicating a logistical or resource management problem with multiple predicates and actions.

## Examining initial state  
- No workflow subjects (collection_batch_1, farm_batch_1, processing_order_1, processing_order_2) are intake_registered or inspection_passed yet, so processing must start from scratch.  
- Collection_batch_allocated_channel and farm_batch_allocated_time_window are already true, and a drying_unit_instance is available from the start.  

## Identifying workflow subjects  
- Workflow subjects include collection_batch_1 and farm_batch_1 (both product_batch) and processing_order_1 and processing_order_2 (work_order_group).  
- All four need to be placed in drying units, with actions like place_collection_batch_into_drying_unit and place_processing_order_into_drying_unit required.  

## Exploring placement preconditions  
- To place workflow subjects, they need release_scheduled, allocated_to_drying_unit, subject_assigned_distribution_slot, and must not already be placed.  
- Release_scheduled requires marked_for_release, which for batches comes from release_collection_batch_for_distribution or release_farm_batch_for_distribution, needing allocation_confirmed and ready.  

## Checking processing order flow  
- Processing_order_1 and processing_order_2 need processing_order_completed to reach marked_for_release, achieved through finalize_order_execution actions.  
- Both orders are already assigned to a unit_instance, but still require reserve_drying_unit_for_subject for allocation.  

## Planning initial steps  
- Start by recording intake for all workflow subjects (e.g., record_intake for collection_batch_1, farm_batch_1) to begin processing.  
- After intake, allocate inspection devices and ensure drying reservations to progress toward inspection_passed for batches.

## Sequencing equipment allocation  
- Need to allocate inspection device (moisture_meter_1) and conditioning equipment (grader_1) sequentially, as they’re shared resources.  
- Release equipment from one batch (e.g., farm_batch_1) before allocating to another (e.g., collection_batch_1) to ensure availability.  

## Reserving time windows  
- Reserve time windows for farm batches (e.g., farm_batch_1 with time_window_1) after inspection passes, ensuring no prior reservations.  
- Effect includes marking the time window as reserved, enabling further steps like confirming batch readiness.  

## Confirming batch readiness  
- Both collection_batch_1 and farm_batch_1 can be confirmed ready after inspection, equipment allocation, and time window reservation.  
- This allows materializing unit instance reservations, linking batches to drying_unit_instance_1 with drying_channel_1 and time_window_1.  

## Managing unit instance configuration  
- Mark drying_unit_instance_1 ready for configuration after ensuring collection_batch_1 is confirmed and moisture_meter_1 is allocated to it.  
- Requires releasing and reallocating inspection devices between batches to meet preconditions like drying_reservation_active.  

## Advancing processing orders  
- Processing orders (processing_order_1, processing_order_2) need intake registration, drying reservations, and inspection passing to proceed.  
- Orders are already assigned to unit instances, but further actions like reserving commodity grades and sequencing equipment are required.

## Handling Shared Resources  
- Both processing_order_1 and processing_order_2 are assigned to drying_unit_instance_1, raising potential conflicts for shared resources.  
- Drying_profile_default and regulatory_doc_1 are single instances; release them from one order (e.g., po1) after use to make them available for the other (e.g., po2).  

## Managing Client Contracts  
- Client_contract_1 is available initially, linked to processing_order_1, while client_contract_2 exists but isn’t listed as available for processing_order_2.  
- The workflow doesn’t require client contracts, avoiding issues with unavailable contracts for po2.  

## Preparing for Distribution  
- Release collection_batch_1 and farm_batch_1 for distribution after they’re confirmed ready and drying_unit_instance_1 is configured, without needing processing orders completed.  
- Distribution slots (dist_slot_1) are limited, allowing only one subject at a time to be scheduled for placement into the drying unit.

## Managing Drying Unit Allocation  
- Only three drying units (drying_unit_a, drying_unit_b, drying_unit_c) are available initially, but four workflow subjects (ws) need processing, creating a resource constraint.  
- Multiple workflow subjects can use the same drying unit sequentially; reserve, process, place (freeing the unit), then reserve again for the next subject, maintaining allocation states per subject-unit pair.  

## Sequencing Workflow Subjects  
- Processing involves reserving a drying unit for each workflow subject, ensuring drying_reservation_active, then completing steps like inspection passing and marking for release.  
- Due to shared resources like drying_profile_default and regulatory_doc_1, workflow subjects must be sequenced, with one released before the next can use them.  

## Handling Batch Distribution  
- Release collection_batch_1 and farm_batch_1 for distribution after drying_unit_instance_1 is ready for configuration, independent of processing order completion.  
- Assigning distribution slots to batches requires them to be marked for release, allocated to moisture_meter_1, and a distribution slot to be available.

## Processing Workflow Steps  
- Orders can complete configuration without requiring a profile if drying_unit_instance_requires_profile_confirmation is false, simplifying the process.  
- Packing requirements are handled during preconfiguration, using preconfigure_order_for_unit_instance for orders without packing needs, ensuring alignment with configuration steps.  

## Configuring Order Sequences  
- Assigning a drying profile is necessary before completing order configuration, ensuring proper setup for processing_order_1 (po1) and processing_order_2 (po2).  
- Completing configuration requires allocation of conditioning equipment (alloc_ce) to the order and confirming order specifications, maintaining resource availability.  

## Handling Client Contracts  
- Assigning client contracts to orders involves inspection passing, allocation, and availability, but the workflow allows bypassing this for orders without available contracts like po2.  
- For po1, client_contract_1 is available, but the path without contracts is used, streamlining the process by skipping contract review and authorization steps.  

## Outlining Batch Processing  
- Start with recording intake for collection_batch_1, then reserve drying_unit_a and allocate inspection devices like moisture_meter_1 for quality confirmation.  
- Process farm_batch_1 similarly, releasing and reallocating resources like moisture_meter_1 and grader_1 between batches to manage constraints effectively.

## Validating Workflow Conditions  
- Preconfiguration for processing_order_1 (po1) and po2 is complete, with all preconditions like allocated conditioning equipment satisfied.  
- For po2, commodity grade is grade_b, and no additional allocations are needed for authorization, ensuring smooth progression.  

## Managing Resource Releases  
- After completing configuration for po1, conditioning equipment was released before authorization, which doesn’t require it, optimizing resource use.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
